texto: What matters in Politics is what men actually do - sincerity is no excuse for acting unpolitically and insincerity may be channelled by politics into good results.
explicacao: Citação de Bernard Crick sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “What matters in Politics is what men actually do - sincerity is no excuse for acting unpoliticall…” articula reflexao, politics, matters em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: Man's inclination to justice makes democracy possible; but man's capacity for injustice makes it necessary.' The optimism we need to prevent ourselves from destroying our own democratic freedoms and, indeed, our own human habitat must be based on reasoned pessimism.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Bernard Crick sobre temas ligados a desenvolvimento pessoal. A afirmação “Man's inclination to justice makes democracy possible; but man's capacity for injustice makes it …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a desenvolvimento-pessoal, democracy, justice, útil em momentos de justice.

texto: Revolutions as often take place because the old regime simply collapse out of economic inefficiency and bureaucratic rigidity rather than for the reasons given out by their successors taking too much credit, however heroic their actions at the time of crisis (but so often in the past hopeless).
explicacao: Citação de Bernard Crick sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Revolutions as often take place because the old regime simply collapse out of economic inefficien…” articula sucesso, bureaucracy, economy em registro de sucesso, com relevância para contextos como economy e revolution.

texto: Monarchy is like a splendid ship, with all sails set it moves majestically on, but then it hits a rock and sinks for ever. Democracy is like a raft. It never sinks but, damn it, your feet are always in the water. That is a good metaphor, for raft, he implies, is simply swept along by the tide or the current; one can with a paddle or a plank steer a little to stay afloat, trim forward direction slightly to left or right, perhaps even slow down or speed up a little, but there is no turning back against the current of democracy.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Bernard Crick: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Monarchy is like a splendid ship, with all sails set it moves majestically on, but then it hits a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, democracy, sinks pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao.

It never sinks but, damn it, your feet are always in the water. That is a good metaphor, for raft, he implies, is simply swept along by the tide or the current; one can with a paddle or a plank steer a little to stay afloat, trim forward direction slightly to left or right, perhaps even slow down or speed up a little, but there is no turning back against the current of democracy.
